Petition For An Order Vacating Beneficiary Designations On Certain Retirement Accounts Of Decedent And Declaring Them Assets Of This Estate
Off Calendar May-05-2017 Denied

Petition For An Order Vacating Beneficiary Designations On Certain Retirement Accounts Of Decedent And Declaring Them Assets Of This Estate
Off Calendar Mar-28-2017 Continued To May-05-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Lidia Colman.
Richard Sopp appeared by telephone for Suzette Allen (Objector).

Counsel stated that the matter is now ready for a half day trial (three hours/9:00 to 12:00). The Petition for an Order Vacating Beneficiary Designations on Certain Retirement Accounts of Decedent and Declaring Them Assets of the Estate is set for trial on May 5, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel are to provide the Court with factual stipulations, witness lists, exhibit lists and any trial briefs the parties wish to present by April 26, 2017.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For An Order Vacating Beneficiary Designations On Certain Retirement Accounts Of Decedent And Declaring Them Assets Of This Estate
Off Calendar Jan-24-2017 Continued To Mar-28-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brian McCaffrey for Lidia Colman.
Richard Sopp appeared by telephone for Sue Allen.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than March 17, 2017. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 22, 2017. The Petition for Order Vacating Beneficiary Designations on Certain Retirement Accounts of Decedent, etc. was continued to and the Parties are to return on March 28, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. It is up to the Mediator to determine if Mr. Sopp's Client will be allowed to participate via telephone. The Court is not setting a trial date at this time. The parties are to submit a joint statement if possible, or separate statements as to what is left, how many witnesses, the length of the trial, etc.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.
